summary: The world is going through a major reset, geopolitically, economically, and financially. Wars are no longer just about armies and weapons; they are about chips, commodities, supply chains, currencies, and narratives. For investors, this changes how risk, returns, and diversification should be understood.

seo.title: Why Silver Is Suddenly Expensive | War, Chips & Global Supply Explained

takeaways.text: The episode argues wars are no longer only about armies: today's conflicts run through chips, commodities, supply chains, currencies and narratives, which changes how investors should read risk and diversification.
Silver and other metals have become strategically important because of their place in technology and global supply chains, and that shift is part of the story behind rising prices.

This is a framework-driven conversation, not a prediction: guest Karan Datta, with over 25 years in wealth management, makes the case that risk control will matter more than returns in the coming decade.
Chasing short-term returns can quietly destroy wealth, so asset allocation, fixed income and multi-asset strategies are offered as practical ways to keep risk in check.
Global investing is in focus, but the conversation separates real diversification from recency bias, and reframes financial freedom as something beyond the numbers.
